sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The detail panel is a slide-out drawer that shows everything about a selected node: its identity, documentation, dFlow annotations, test coverage, and change history.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
1. Click any node in the graph or any row in the list view
2. The detail panel slides in from the right
3. Scroll through sections to see metadata, docs, steps, tests, and history
4. Click the X button or press Escape to close

sections.heading: Panel Sections
sections.content:
- **Type & Staleness:** Colored badges at the top show node type and current staleness status
- **ID:** Full cortex node ID with a copy button
- **Location:** Source file path (click copy to use in other tools)
- **Tags:** All tags assigned to this node
- **Summary:** One-line summary (level_0)
- **Description:** Full docstring rendered as markdown (level_1)
- **Autodoc:** Extended documentation (level_2) when available
- **Verification:** Shows VERIFIED status; for DESC_STALE nodes, a "Mark Clean" button appears
- **Steps:** If the node is a dFlow workflow/task/test, shows the step table
- **Tests:** Test results for this node (pass/fail/skip with durations)
- **History:** Timeline of changes (INITIAL, CODE_ONLY, DESC_ONLY, etc.) with timestamps and git SHAs

sections.heading: Actions
sections.content:
| Action | Effect |
|---|---|
| Copy ID | Copies the full node ID to clipboard |
| Copy Location | Copies the source file path |
| Mark Clean | Records a manual verification for DESC_STALE nodes |
| Close (X) | Hides the panel and deselects the node |

sections.heading: Tips
sections.content:
- Use the node ID copy button to quickly reference a node in CLI commands like `cortex graph <id> .`
- The Mark Clean button only appears for DESC_STALE nodes — CONTENT_STALE and STRUCTURAL_DRIFT require code/doc changes
- History is sorted newest-first; the most recent change is at the top
